J1.basis: the SaaS pivoted from InboxZap (email cleanup) to Lumina (AI meeting assistant), and the repo hadn't caught up... GSC shows the site is still living almost entirely off InboxZap-era brand and topic queries
J1.rationale: Explicitly names all three states — live site = meeting assistant, GSC = legacy inbox demand, repo = stale — and frames every recommendation around ranking on-topic content while not chasing dead inbox-cleanup topics.

J2.basis: queries like 'gmail storage full,' 'mass delete emails,' 'outlook sweep feature' get real impressions but 0-1 clicks at position 40-65... where a post has a plausible bridge... add one contextual link/CTA into Lumina; where there's no bridge, leave as historical content
J2.rationale: Applies data-justified tiers (harvest brand equity, bridge overlapping posts, keep off-topic as historical) with cited impressions/positions, but stops short of an explicit disposition per each of the 12 posts using their individual numbers — the treatment is largely blanket 'reconnect + keep all,' with bridges on only two.

J3.basis: Don't invest in ranking these further — it dilutes topical relevance for 'AI meeting assistant'... where a post has a plausible bridge... add one contextual link/CTA into Lumina; where there's no bridge, leave as historical content but stop treating it as a growth lever
J3.rationale: Explicitly names the tension and lands a defensible middle: reconnect/preserve indexed equity and capture live brand demand via a rebrand bridge, while refusing to chase dead-topic impressions and building the meeting-assistant cluster as the growth engine — avoiding both failure modes.
